methods.description: Process for analyzing stable isotope measurements from coral skeletal powder samples to determine isotopic values.

steps.description: Stable isotope measurements were analyzed individually at 0.5 mm intervals at the MARUM-Center for Marine Environmental Sciences of the University of Bremen, Bremen, Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stable isotope measurements at 0.5 mm intervals

steps.description: For each coral skeletal powder sample, approximately 60-120 μg of powder was dissolved in ∼105% H3PO4 at 75°C in an automated carbonate preparation device, generating CO2 gas that was then analyzed on an isotope-ratio mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve coral powder in H3PO4 and analyze CO2 gas on mass spectrometer

steps.description: Calibration and correction of offsets due to inter-machine differences were based on measurements of the Solnhofen limestone 2008 in-house standard, which itself was calibrated against NBS 19.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate and correct isotope measurements using Solnhofen limestone standard

steps.description: The isotopic values are reported in ‰ versus the Vienna Peedee Belemnite reference scale.
steps.searchable_summary: report isotopic values in ‰ versus Vienna Peedee Belemnite scale

methods.description: Procedure for measuring the elemental composition of coral skeletal powder using ICP-MS.

steps.description: Ultrapure 2% HNO3 (2 mL) was added to digest 500 μg of coral skeletal powder, shaken and dissolved for at least 24 hr.
steps.searchable_summary: digest coral powder with 2% HNO3 for 24 hours

steps.description: An aliquot of 0.6 mL from the initial solution was diluted further with 0.6 mL of 2% ultrapure HNO3 to reach a calcium concentration of approximately 50 ppm.
steps.searchable_summary: dilute coral solution to achieve 50 ppm calcium concentration

steps.description: Certified single element stock solutions from Inorganic Ventures were used for the calibration of each individual element.
steps.searchable_summary: calibrate elemental analysis using certified stock solutions

steps.description: Five calibration solutions of different concentrations for the different elements except Ca were prepared from one stock solution that was used over 1 week.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare calibration solutions for elemental analysis

steps.description: After every fifth sample measurement on the ICP-MS, brackets of laboratory standards were measured to correct for instrumental drift.
steps.searchable_summary: measure laboratory standards to correct for instrumental drift

methods.description: Method for collecting and preparing coral samples for geochemical analysis.

steps.description: In July 2005, a 53 cm long coral core was collected from the center growth axis of a S. siderea colony off the northern coast of Havana, Cuba at 6 m depth.
steps.searchable_summary: collect coral core from S. siderea colony

steps.description: Slabs measuring 5 mm were removed from the coral core half with X-radiograph imaging completed to reveal internal growth structure to guide sampling transect selection.
steps.searchable_summary: use X-radiograph imaging to guide coral slab sampling

steps.description: The individual slabs were cleaned in an ultrasonic bath with 18.2 Ω milliQ water.
steps.searchable_summary: clean coral slabs in ultrasonic bath

steps.description: A continuous theca wall of an individual coral polyp identified on the X-ray images was microsampled by milling for skeletal powder material.
steps.searchable_summary: microsample coral theca wall for skeletal powder

methods.method_name: Age Model Comparison of δ 18 O and Sr/Ca With Instrumental SST
methods.description: Development and comparison of age models based on δ 18 O and Sr/Ca with instrumental sea surface temperature (SST) data.

steps.description: Developed two different age models based on δ 18 O and the more established Sr/Ca.
steps.searchable_summary: develop age models using δ 18 O and Sr/Ca data

steps.description: The pseudo coral model shows a correlation coefficient between the model and OI-SSTv2.1 of 0.98, indicating a stronger influence of SST on the corals δ 18 O.
steps.searchable_summary: validate age model with OI-SSTv2.1 data

steps.description: Verification of our coral-based δ 18 O-SST reconstruction with the gridded ER-SST yields a correlation coefficient of (r) = 0.54 for the period 1854-2005.
steps.searchable_summary: verify δ 18 O-SST reconstruction with ER-SST data
